Early Power BI sales dashboard with dynamic slicers, KPI cards, and multiple chart types.
Supermarket sales Power BI dashboard screenshot

One of my first Power BI projects, analyzing supermarket sales with KPI cards (total sales, profit, top product/category), stacked columns, bar/pie/area/tree charts, and slicers for month, year, sales type, and payment mode.

Tableau dashboard for UK regional banking data with interactive slicers and cross-filtering.

Created a regional banking dashboard in Tableau using data from Scotland, Northern Ireland, England, and Wales. Includes customer age and balance distributions, gender breakdown, and job profiles—with slicers for age, balance, and gender plus cross-filtering for dynamic exploration of banking trends and demographics.

Designed and deployed 20+ operational analytics solutions for infrastructure monitoring, disaster recovery, SLA management, and workforce operations.
20+ operational analytics solutions for infrastructure and workforce ops
Disaster management analytics for prioritized field response
Critical alarms monitoring contributing to zero fire incidents
Analytics for 8 Bn LKR annual electricity budget optimization
Battery health and backup monitoring reducing critical downtime
Workforce and SLA dashboards for regional operations

## Context

In a regional operations analytics role for a national telecom infrastructure provider, I built operational intelligence systems supporting field teams, disaster recovery, and senior management planning.

## Scope

Analytics covered telecom infrastructure monitoring, disaster recovery coordination, SLA management, workforce operations, electricity budget optimization, and infrastructure reliability—including critical alarms and risk analytics.

## Approach

1. **Operational monitoring** — Dashboards for real-time infrastructure health, alarms, and escalation tracking.
2. **Disaster management** — Prioritization analytics for field response and resource utilization during large-scale failures.
3. **Risk & reliability** — Battery health, design backup monitoring, and critical alarms to reduce downtime and safety incidents.
4. **Workforce & SLA** — Task completion, escalation, and regional visibility for operations teams.
5. **Executive support** — Evidence and insights for senior management on resource allocation and infrastructure planning.

## Solution structure

| Domain | Analytics focus |
|--------|-----------------|
| **Infrastructure** | Site monitoring, alarms, battery/backup health, reliability KPIs |
| **Disaster recovery** | Prioritized response, resource optimization during outages |
| **Energy** | Electricity spend analytics for multi-billion LKR operational budget |
| **Workforce** | SLA tracking, task completion, escalation workflows |
| **Safety** | Critical alarms and risk analytics supporting incident prevention |

## Outcomes

Delivered 20+ solutions improving operational visibility across regional teams. Disaster management systems enabled faster, data-driven field response. Electricity analytics supported optimized use of a large annual operational budget. Infrastructure risk analytics contributed to achieving zero fire incidents and reduced critical downtime scenarios.

Data owner for 20+ Power BI solutions spanning supply chain, logistics, inventory, production, and finance—with SAP integration and AI-assisted development workflows.
Data owner for 20+ reporting solutions used from operational teams through Group CEO
SAP integration replacing manual Excel-based reporting workflows
Warehouse KPIs, Tobacco Budget, Cigar Composition, and Tobacco Duration analytics
Calculation Groups, Field Parameters, and Row-Level Security across enterprise reports
AI-assisted workflows for DAX authoring, debugging, governance, and documentation

## Context

As Power BI Engineer for a global manufacturing and supply chain organization, I serve as the primary data owner for enterprise operational reporting. Solutions span supply chain, logistics, inventory, production, finance, and group-level operations.

## Scope

Reporting domains include end-to-end operational visibility: warehouse performance, bill-of-materials analysis, inventory and consumption forecasting, procurement planning, and executive group dashboards. Stakeholders range from shop-floor supervisors to the Group CEO.

## Approach

1. **Requirements** — Lead workshops with business stakeholders; translate needs into technical specifications and scope documents.
2. **Design** — Proof-of-concepts and interactive mockups to validate data models and UX before build.
3. **Standards** — UI/UX design standards for consistency across enterprise reports.
4. **Build** — Semantic modeling, advanced DAX, report design, performance tuning, and deployment.
5. **Governance** — Workspace governance, refresh scheduling, security roles, RLS, and cross-environment deployment.
6. **Validation** — Data accuracy checks across frontend reports, backend sources, and query logic.

## Solution structure

| Layer | Focus |
|-------|--------|
| **Sources** | SAP operational data integrated into automated pipelines (replacing manual Excel workflows) |
| **Model** | Scalable star schemas, optimized refresh pipelines, advanced DAX for high-volume operational reporting |
| **Reports** | Domain-specific dashboards: Warehouse KPIs, Tobacco Budget, Cigar Composition, Tobacco Duration |
| **Features** | Calculation Groups, Field Parameters, RLS, Power BI Service workflows |
| **Delivery** | Managed workspaces, scheduled refresh, role-based access for managers through executive level |

## AI-assisted development

Agentic coding with Cursor and Claude Code accelerates report development, DAX authoring, debugging, version control, documentation, and data transformation automation—while maintaining governance and quality standards.

## Outcomes

Solutions are actively used for data-driven decision making across operations and leadership. SAP automation reduced reporting effort and turnaround time. Advanced Power BI patterns ensure consistency, security, and performance at enterprise scale.
